# API-Contract Requirements Quality Checklist: Atomicity-test router (read-only classifier) (PRSG-007)

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements defining the emitted JSON contract (the routing decision) are complete, clear, consistent, and stable enough for the downstream PRSG-008 layer-planner to parse — before implementation
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command. Every item is a "unit test for the requirements" — it interrogates the WRITING of the contract requirements (completeness / clarity / consistency / measurability / coverage) across `spec.md`, `data-model.md`, and `contracts/routing-decision.schema.json`, NOT the behavior of any code.

**Focus areas (from request)**:

1. The JSON contract surface: `route` enum, `releasable` boolean, `signals[]` / `hints[]` arrays, `warnings[]` array.
2. Field-name and enum stability so PRSG-008 (layer-planner) can parse it as a fixed contract.
3. Alignment with the existing `reviewability-gate.sh` JSON-shape conventions (flat top-level keys, string arrays).
4. The route precedence ladder being unambiguous and total.

## Contract Field-Set Completeness

- [ ] CHK026 Is the full success-object field set (`route`, `releasable`, `signals`, `hints`, `warnings`) enumerated in the requirements with each field's type stated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a, data-model Entity 1]
- [ ] CHK027 Is the error-object field set (`{"error": <string>}`, no `route` key) specified as a distinct shape from the success object, so the two are never conflated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a, §Key Entities/Routing decision]
- [ ] CHK028 Is it specified which fields are REQUIRED on the success path (all five) versus which arrays MAY be empty, so a consumer knows an absent key is a contract violation rather than an optional omission? [Completeness, data-model Entity 1, schema `required`]
- [ ] CHK029 Is it stated that the success object and the error object are mutually exclusive (exactly one of `route` / `error` is present, never both)? [Completeness, schema `oneOf`, Spec §FR-011a]
- [x] CHK030 Does the requirement set bound what may appear in `signals[]` to a single closed, namespaced vocabulary AND keep that vocabulary consistent across spec, data model, and the JSON schema? [Conflict, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2, schema `signals.items`] — RESOLVED: schema `signals.items` was a CLOSED 7-value `enum` that excluded the US1 additive-multi-seam / modify-heavy routing token that FR-011b and data-model Entity 2 both place in `signals[]`; a compliant `modify-heavy` output (`signals:["<US1 modify-heavy token>"]`) would have failed schema validation. Loosened `signals.items` to an `anyOf` admitting the 7 decisive tokens OR the deferred US1 routing namespace (pattern-matched, spelling fixed at implement time per FR-011b). Schema `description` updated to name the US1 read as a member of the controlled vocabulary. (See "Unresolved" — the exact US1 token spelling is a deferred implement-time decision; flagged for consensus.)

## Route Enum Stability & Completeness

- [ ] CHK031 Is the `route` enum specified as a fixed set of exactly five values (`split-PR`, `one-navigable-PR`, `single-atomic-PR`, `out-of-scope`, `branch-by-abstraction`) consistently across spec, data model, and schema? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, data-model `route` enum, schema enum]
- [ ] CHK032 Is the reserved-never-emitted status of `branch-by-abstraction` stated unambiguously (it stays in the enum as a stable contract value but the MVP MUST NOT emit it)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, §SC-008, data-model Entity 1]
- [ ] CHK033 Is the rationale for keeping `branch-by-abstraction` in the enum (rather than dropping it) documented, so a future maintainer does not "clean up" the unused value and break the PRSG-008 contract? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §Out of Scope]
- [ ] CHK034 Is the enum's stability as a downstream contract for PRSG-008 stated explicitly (field names AND enum values are a stable contract), so a value rename is recognized as a breaking change? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011a, §FR-011b]
- [ ] CHK035 Are the conditions that map a change to each emittable route value (`split-PR`, `one-navigable-PR`, `single-atomic-PR`, `out-of-scope`) each specified, so every emittable value has a defined trigger? [Completeness, data-model Entity 4, §FR-003]

## Route Precedence Ladder — Unambiguity & Totality

- [ ] CHK036 Is the precedence ladder specified as TOTAL (every governed input maps to exactly one route) and ORDERED (a deterministic order resolves conflicting signals)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, §Edge Cases/Conflicting signals]
- [ ] CHK037 Is the first rung — input-shape short-circuit (missing/empty `tasks.md` → `out-of-scope` BEFORE any detector, including the hard-atomic override) — specified unambiguously as running first? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, §Edge Cases/Conflicting signals]
- [ ] CHK038 Is the override relationship specified unambiguously (a hard-atomic signature yields `single-atomic-PR`, overriding any `split-PR` signal from detected seams)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, §FR-003, §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK039 Is the abstain rung specified as the total fallback (any change not short-circuited, not hard-atomic, and not a proven additive multi-seam → `one-navigable-PR`), leaving no input unrouted? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §Edge Cases/No discernible signal]
- [ ] CHK040 Is it specified that the precedence ladder and the detector run-order (the five detectors in FR-003 order) are consistent with one another, so the documented decision order matches the documented detector order? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, research D3]
- [x] CHK041 Does the requirement set guarantee the ladder produces NO tie (no input can satisfy two route-producing rungs without the order resolving it deterministically)? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003] — RESOLVED (already satisfied): FR-003 + the "Conflicting signals / precedence" edge case state the precedence is "total and ordered" with an explicit 1→2→3→4 numbering, and FR-007/FR-008 make releasability orthogonal to (not competing with) the route, so no two route-producing rungs can fire without the order breaking the tie. No edit needed; item documents the coverage.

## `releasable` Boolean — Definition & Orthogonality

- [ ] CHK042 Is the `releasable` boolean's truth condition specified exactly (`false` iff destructive-migration OR concurrency class; `true` otherwise)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, §FR-009, data-model Entity 1]
- [ ] CHK043 Is the orthogonality of `releasable` to `route` stated explicitly (a change MAY be `single-atomic-PR` AND `releasable: false`), so a consumer never infers releasability from the route value? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, data-model Entity 4]
- [ ] CHK044 Is the linkage between `releasable: false` and a mandatory `warnings[]` entry specified (every not-releasable result carries exactly the matching canonical warning), so the boolean and the warning can never disagree? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, data-model "Validation rules summary"]

## `signals[]` vs `hints[]` Separation & Vocabulary

- [ ] CHK045 Is the `signals[]` controlled vocabulary specified as a closed, stable, namespaced token set (the seven decisive `hard-atomic:*` / `releasability:*` tokens plus the US1 routing read), distinct from free-form text? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2]
- [ ] CHK046 Is the disjointness of `signals[]` and `hints[]` stated as an invariant (the three advisory probes emit ONLY into `hints[]` and MUST NEVER appear in `signals[]`)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2]
- [ ] CHK047 Is the US1 additive-multi-seam vs modify-heavy routing read specified as a `signals[]` member (a decisive finding) rather than a `hints[]` member, and is its token required to be stable and non-colliding with the `hard-atomic:` / `releasability:` namespaces? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2 "US1 routing reads"]
- [x] CHK048 Is the exact token spelling for every `signals[]` member defined, OR is any deferred spelling explicitly marked as deferred with a stability constraint so PRSG-008 is not left guessing? [Gap, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2] — RESOLVED: the seven `hard-atomic:*` / `releasability:*` tokens are spelled verbatim in FR-011b and data-model Entity 2; the US1 routing-read token spelling is explicitly deferred to implement time WITH a stability + namespace-non-collision constraint stated in data-model Entity 2. The deferral is now mirrored in the schema (CHK030 fix). The token-naming decision itself is flagged for consensus (see "Unresolved") so PRSG-008's parse contract is locked before that spec begins.
- [ ] CHK049 Is it specified that `warnings[]` carries ONLY the human CI-green sentences (no detector tokens, no advisory-probe output), keeping the three arrays' payloads non-overlapping? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 3]

## `warnings[]` Canonical Strings — Exactness & Consistency

- [ ] CHK050 Are the two canonical `warnings[]` sentences specified as exact fixed strings (not paraphrasable), so the schema enum and the prose cannot drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, data-model Entity 3, schema `warnings.items`]
- [ ] CHK051 Are the two warning strings BYTE-IDENTICAL across the three sources that define them (spec §FR-008, data-model Entity 3, and the schema `warnings.items` enum)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, data-model Entity 3, schema] — verified identical (incl. the `≠` glyph and parenthetical) across all three sources.
- [ ] CHK052 Is each warning string bound to exactly one releasability class (destructive-migration ↔ its sentence; concurrency ↔ its sentence), so a class can never emit the wrong canonical warning? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, data-model Entity 3]

## Alignment with `reviewability-gate.sh` Conventions

- [ ] CHK053 Is the requirement to follow the existing `reviewability-gate.sh` JSON conventions (flat top-level keys, string arrays, top-level `{"error":...}` on the error path) stated explicitly as the source of the contract's shape? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011a, research D2]
- [ ] CHK054 Is the deliberate DIVERGENCE from the gate documented (the classifier has no `blockers[]`/`status: block` and never exits 1, because it is advisory-only), so the alignment is understood as "same shape minus the blocking outcome" rather than identical? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, §Assumptions/Exit-status contract, research D1]
- [ ] CHK055 Is it specified that the contract intentionally omits any LOC/sizing field the gate carries (no `reviewable_loc`, `production_files`, `thresholds`, etc.), consistent with the no-sizing requirement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, §Out of Scope]

## Contract Stability for PRSG-008 (Downstream Parseability)

- [ ] CHK056 Is the machine-readable contract pinned in a single authoritative artifact (`contracts/routing-decision.schema.json`) that the prose points to, so PRSG-008 has one canonical source to parse against? [Completeness, plan §Project Structure, data-model intro]
- [ ] CHK057 Is the schema kept consistent with the prose on the success/error split, the field set, the route enum, the `signals[]` vocabulary, and the `warnings[]` strings (no schema-vs-prose drift)? [Consistency, schema vs Spec §FR-011a/§FR-011b/§FR-008] — drift audit performed: route enum SET matches (order immaterial); both `warnings[]` strings byte-identical; the `signals.items` closed-enum drift (CHK030) was the one defect, now reconciled.
- [ ] CHK058 Is `additionalProperties: false` (or an equivalent "no extra keys" requirement) specified so PRSG-008 can treat the contract as closed and an unexpected key as a violation? [Completeness, schema `additionalProperties`, Spec §FR-011a]
- [ ] CHK059 Is a change-control expectation for this contract stated (field names and enum values are STABLE; a rename/removal is a breaking change owned by a named downstream spec), so the contract is not silently mutated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011a, §FR-011b, §Out of Scope]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK060 Is the assumption that PRSG-008 (and the Layer-4 fixtures) treat `signals[]` as a closed contract documented, so the cost of changing the vocabulary is visible? [Assumption, Spec §FR-011b, data-model Entity 2]
- [ ] CHK061 Is the assumption documented that the change class is NOT a separate emitted field (it is recoverable from `route` + `signals[]`), so a consumer does not expect a `change_class` key? [Assumption, Spec §Out of Scope, §Key Entities/Change class, data-model Entity 4]

## Notes

- An **unchecked** `- [ ]` item is a SATISFIED (passing) requirements-quality test — the spec/data-model/schema already meet it; the item documents the coverage. A **checked** `- [x]` item is one that surfaced a defect (`[Gap]`/`[Conflict]`) that has since been REMEDIATED (resolution noted inline). No unchecked item is an open issue.
- Items marked `[Gap]` / `[Conflict]` indicated a requirements-writing or contract-drift issue; all such items are now resolved (checked) with the fix annotated inline, EXCEPT where the inline note explicitly defers a decision to consensus (the US1 `signals[]` token spelling, CHK030 / CHK048).
- This checklist tests the QUALITY of the contract requirements, not the behavior of the script.
- Item numbering continues from the sibling checklists in this directory (`requirements.md`, `error-handling.md`); IDs start at CHK026 to remain globally unique within the feature.
- Decided context (do not reopen): flat top-level `route` / `releasable` / `signals` / `hints` / `warnings`, error path `{"error":...}` (FR-011a); controlled `signals[]` vocabulary (FR-011b); five-value `route` enum with `branch-by-abstraction` RESERVED-never-emitted; precedence ladder out-of-scope → single-atomic-PR → split-PR → one-navigable-PR (FR-003); two fixed releasability warning sentences (FR-008).
